title: Add and subtract mixed numbers with unlike denominators (no regrouping)
description: Practice adding and subtracting mixed numbers with different denominators. No regrouping required.
topic_path: Adding and subtracting mixed numbers with unlike denominators
Add and subtract mixed numbers with unlike denominators (no regrouping)

hints:
Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by adding each piece. 

$ \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{4}} + \blueD4 \maroonD{\dfrac{2}{5}}$

First, let's add the $\maroonE{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueE{0} &\maroonE{\dfrac{1}{4}}\\\\
+&\blueE{4}&\maroonE{\dfrac{2}{5}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

Our denominators need to be the same so we can add. 

What is the least common multiple for the denominators $\maroonE4$ and $\maroonE5$?

Now, we can add our $\maroonE{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueE{0} &\maroonE{\dfrac{5}{20}}\\\\
+&\blueE{4}&\maroonE{\dfrac{8}{20}}\\
\hline{}\\
&&\maroonE{\dfrac{13}{20}}
\end{align}$

Next, let's add the $\blueE{\text{whole numbers}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\,\,\blueE{0} &\maroonE{\dfrac{5}{20}}\\\\
+&\,\,\blueE{4}&\maroonE{\dfrac{8}{20}}\\
\hline{}\\
&\,\,\blueE{4}&\maroonE{\dfrac{13}{20}}
\end{align}$
$\maroonE{\dfrac{1}{4}} + \blueE4 \maroonE{\dfrac{2}{5}} =  \blueE{} \maroonE{\dfrac{13}{20}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by adding each piece. 

$\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{3}} + \blueD2 \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{6}}$

First, let's add the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{0} &\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{3}}\\\\
+&\blueD{2}&\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{6}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

Our denominators need to be the same so we can add. 

What is the least common multiple for the denominators $\maroonD3$ and $\maroonD6$?

Now, we can add our $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{0} &\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{6}}\\\\
+&\blueD{2}&\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{6}}\\
\hline{}\\
&&\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{6}}
\end{align}$

Next, let's add the $\blueD{\text{whole numbers}}$.


$\begin{align}
&\blueD{0} &\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{6}}\\\\
+&\blueD{2}&\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{6}}\\
\hline{}\\
&\blueD2&\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{6}}
\end{align}$
$ \maroonD{\dfrac{2}{3}} + \blueD2 \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{6}} =  \blueD{2} \maroonD{\dfrac{5}{6}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by adding each piece. 

$\blueD8 \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{6}} + \maroonD{\dfrac{3}{4}}$

First, let's add the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{8} &\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{6}}\\\\
+&\blueD{0}&\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{4}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

Our denominators need to be the same so we can add. 

What is the least common multiple for the denominators $\maroonD6$ and $\maroonD{4}$?

Now, we can add our $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{8} &\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{12}}\\\\
+&\blueD{0}&\maroonD{\dfrac{9}{12}}\\
\hline{}\\
&&\maroonD{\dfrac{11}{12}}
\end{align}$

Next, let's add the $\blueD{\text{whole numbers}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{8} &\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{12}}\\\\
+&\blueD{0}&\maroonD{\dfrac{9}{12}}\\
\hline{}\\
&\blueD{8}&\maroonD{\dfrac{11}{12}}
\end{align}$
$\blueD8 \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{6}} + \maroonD{\dfrac{3}{4}} = \blueD{8}\maroonD{\dfrac{11}{12}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by adding each piece. 

$\blueD1 \maroonD{\dfrac{3}{8}} + \blueD3 \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{3}}$

First, let's add the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{1} &\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{8}}\\\\
+&\blueD{3}&\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{3}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

Our denominators need to be the same so we can add. 

What is the least common multiple for the denominators $\maroonD8$ and $\maroonD3$?

Now, we can add our $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{1} &\maroonD{\dfrac{9}{24}}\\\\
+&\blueD{3}&\maroonD{\dfrac{8}{24}}\\
\hline{}\\
&&\maroonD{\dfrac{17}{24}}
\end{align}$

Next, let's add the $\blueD{\text{whole numbers}}$.


$\begin{align}
&\blueD{1} &\maroonD{\dfrac{9}{24}}\\\\
+&\blueD{3}&\maroonD{\dfrac{8}{24}}\\
\hline{}\\
&\blueD4&\maroonD{\dfrac{17}{24}}
\end{align}$
$\blueD1 \maroonD{\dfrac{3}{8}} + \blueD3 \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{3}} = \blueD4\maroonD{\dfrac{17}{24}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by adding each piece. 

$\blueD2 \maroonD{\dfrac{2}{7}} + \blueD4 \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{2}}$

First, let's add the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{2} &\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{7}}\\\\
+&\blueD{4}&\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{2}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

Our denominators need to be the same so we can add. 

What is the least common multiple for the denominators $\maroonD7$ and $\maroonD2$?

Now, we can add our $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{2} &\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{14}}\\\\
+&\blueD{4}&\maroonD{\dfrac{7}{14}}\\
\hline{}\\
&&\maroonD{\dfrac{11}{14}}
\end{align}$

Next, let's add the $\blueD{\text{whole numbers}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{2} &\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{14}}\\\\
+&\blueD{4}&\maroonD{\dfrac{7}{14}}\\
\hline{}\\
&\blueD6&\maroonD{\dfrac{11}{14}}
\end{align}$
$\blueD2 \maroonD{\dfrac{2}{7}} + \blueD4 \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{2}} = \blueD6\maroonD{\dfrac{11}{14}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by adding each piece. 

$\blueD3 \maroonD{\dfrac{3}{5}} + \blueD6\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{10}}$

First, let's add the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{3} &\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{5}}\\\\
+&\blueD{6}&\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{10}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

Our denominators need to be the same so we can add. 

What is the least common multiple for the denominators $\maroonD5$ and $\maroonD{10}$?

Now, we can add our $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{3} &\maroonD{\dfrac{6}{10}}\\\\
+&\blueD{6}&\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{10}}\\
\hline{}\\
&&\maroonD{\dfrac{9}{10}}
\end{align}$

Next, let's add the $\blueD{\text{whole numbers}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{3} &\maroonD{\dfrac{6}{10}}\\\\
+&\blueD{6}&\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{10}}\\
\hline{}\\
&\blueD9&\maroonD{\dfrac{9}{10}}
\end{align}$
$\blueD3 \maroonD{\dfrac{3}{5}} + \blueD6\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{10}} = \blueD9\maroonD{\dfrac{9}{10}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by adding each piece. 

$\blueD5\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{3}} + \blueD7\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{4}}$

First, let's add the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{5} &\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{3}}\\\\
+&\blueD{7}&\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{4}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

Our denominators need to be the same so we can add. 

What is the least common multiple for the denominators $\maroonD3$ and $\maroonD{4}$?

Now, we can add our $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{5} &\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{12}}\\\\
+&\blueD{7}&\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{12}}\\
\hline{}\\
&&\maroonD{\dfrac{7}{12}}
\end{align}$

Next, let's add the $\blueD{\text{whole numbers}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{5} &\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{12}}\\\\
+&\blueD{7}&\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{12}}\\
\hline{}\\
&\blueD{12}&\maroonD{\dfrac{7}{12}}
\end{align}$
$\blueD5\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{3}} + \blueD7\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{4}} = \blueD{12}\maroonD{\dfrac{7}{12}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by adding each piece. 

$\blueD4\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{2}} + \blueD1\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{5}}$

First, let's add the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{4} &\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{2}}\\\\
+&\blueD{1}&\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{5}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

Our denominators need to be the same so we can add. 

What is the least common multiple for the denominators $\maroonD2$ and $\maroonD{5}$?

Now, we can add our $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{4} &\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{10}}\\\\
+&\blueD{1}&\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{10}}\\
\hline{}\\
&&\maroonD{\dfrac{9}{10}}
\end{align}$

Next, let's add the $\blueD{\text{whole numbers}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{4} &\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{10}}\\\\
+&\blueD{1}&\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{10}}\\
\hline{}\\
&\blueD{5}&\maroonD{\dfrac{9}{10}}
\end{align}$
$\blueD4\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{2}} + \blueD1\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{5}} = \blueD{5}\maroonD{\dfrac{9}{10}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by subtracting each piece. 

$\blueD2 \maroonD{\dfrac{3}{4}} -  \maroonD{\dfrac{5}{16}}$

First, let's subtract the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{2} &\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{4}}\\\\
-&\blueD{0}&\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{16}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

Our denominators need to be the same so we can subtract. 

What is the least common multiple for the denominators $\maroonD4$ and $\maroonD{16}$?

Now, we can subtract our $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{2} &\maroonD{\dfrac{12}{16}}\\\\
-&\blueD{0}&\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{16}}\\
\hline{}\\
&&\maroonD{\dfrac{7}{16}}
\end{align}$

Next, let's subtract the $\blueD{\text{whole numbers}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{2} &\maroonD{\dfrac{12}{16}}\\\\
-&\blueD{0}&\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{16}}\\
\hline{}\\
&\blueD2&\maroonD{\dfrac{7}{16}}
\end{align}$
$\blueD2 \maroonD{\dfrac{3}{4}} - \maroonD{\dfrac{5}{16}} = \blueD2 \maroonD{\dfrac{7}{16}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by subtracting each piece. 

$\blueD5\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{3}} - \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{4}}$

First, let's subtract the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{5} &\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{3}}\\\\
-&\blueD{0}&\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{4}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

Our denominators need to be the same so we can subtract. 

What is the least common multiple for the denominators $\maroonD3$ and $\maroonD{4}$?

Now, we can subtract our $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{5} &\maroonD{\dfrac{8}{12}}\\\\
-&\blueD{0}&\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{12}}\\
\hline{}\\
&&\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{12}}
\end{align}$

Next, let's subtract the $\blueD{\text{whole numbers}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{5} &\maroonD{\dfrac{8}{12}}\\\\
-&\blueD{0}&\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{12}}\\
\hline{}\\
&\blueD5&\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{12}}
\end{align}$
$\blueD5\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{3}} - \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{4}} = \blueD5\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{12}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by subtracting each piece. 

$\blueD8\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{5}} - \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{2}}$

First, let's subtract the $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{8} &\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{5}}\\\\
-&\blueD{0}&\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{2}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

Our denominators need to be the same so we can subtract. 

What is the least common multiple for the denominators $\maroonD5$ and $\maroonD{2}$?

Now, we can subtract our $\maroonD{\text{fractions}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{8} &\maroonD{\dfrac{8}{10}}\\\\
-&\blueD{0}&\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{10}}\\
\hline{}\\
&&\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{10}}
\end{align}$

Next, let's subtract the $\blueD{\text{whole numbers}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{8} &\maroonD{\dfrac{8}{10}}\\\\
-&\blueD{0}&\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{10}}\\
\hline{}\\
&\blueD8&\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{10}}
\end{align}$
$\blueD8\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{5}} - \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{2}} = \blueD8\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{10}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by subtracting each piece.

-&\blueD{2}&\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{10}}\\

&\blueD5&\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{10}}

$\blueD7\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{5}} - \blueD2\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{10}} = \blueD5\maroonD{\dfrac{3}{10}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by subtracting each piece. 

$\blueD4\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{6}} - \blueD1\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{3}}$

\end{align}$

Our denominators need to be the same so we can subtract. 

What is the least common multiple for the denominators $\maroonD6$ and $\maroonD{3}$?

&\blueD{4} &\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{6}}\\\\

&&\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{6}}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{4} &\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{6}}\\\\
-&\blueD{1}&\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{6}}\\
\hline{}\\
&\blueD3&\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{6}}
\end{align}$
$\blueD4\maroonD{\dfrac{5}{6}} - \blueD1\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{3}} = \blueD3\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{6}}$

Each mixed number has a $\blueD{\text{whole number}}$ and a $\maroonD{\text{fraction}}$. Let's start by subtracting each piece.

&\blueD{6} &\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{5}}\\\\
-&\blueD{3}&\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{4}}\\
\hline{}\\
\end{align}$

Our denominators need to be the same so we can subtract. 

What is the least common multiple for the denominators $\maroonD5$ and $\maroonD{4}$?

$\begin{align}
&\blueD{6} &\maroonD{\dfrac{8}{20}}\\\\
